author: tj.jackson@gmail.com
body: Rebecca, can you confirm Rev. Dawson's preferred audio format for the pre-interview? She mentioned she may call in rather than come to the studio.

author: rebecca.price@memorialhospital-mem.org
body: Confirmed - she'll call in on the studio landline. TJ, can you set up the delay board for the January 3 slot? I'll do a brief intro from the studio and hand off to her. She's very prepared.

author: rebecca.price@memorialhospital-mem.org
body: Adding Sandra Coles to the confirmed guest list for February 7. She'll speak to nursing as a vocation and compassion fatigue - perfect tie-in for our winter wellness focus. Her Bluff City Community College email is on file; I'll communicate through personal email (sandra.coles@gmail.com).

author: tj.jackson@gmail.com
body: Covenant Insurance Group renewed their annual spot - they asked if we can move their read to the first break instead of the second. Thoughts?

author: rebecca.price@memorialhospital-mem.org
body: Fine with me - first break works better flow-wise anyway. Let Mitchell's Flowers know they're moving to second break slot for the Honoring the Women of Faith episodes. I'll update the run-of-show.

author: lorraine.vickers@gmail.com
body: Rebecca, the APC is accepting early registrations for the fall national conference. With 22 hours still needed by year end this would be a solid investment. Let me know if you'd like me to forward the details to admin for reimbursement approval.

author: rebecca.price@memorialhospital-mem.org
body: Yes, please forward. I'd like to register before March 1 to get the early-bird rate. I'll plan around the Saturday broadcast - the conference is mid-week so it should be workable.

author: lorraine.vickers@gmail.com
body: Good developmental notes here. For Resident 2 - consider pairing them with a second long-term palliative family before the next supervision check-in. Boundary coaching lands better with a live case to reference.

author: rebecca.price@memorialhospital-mem.org
body: Agreed. I'll arrange that in the next rotation. I want to be careful not to push them into complexity before they've got the reflective cycle solid - will keep an eye on it in our Wednesday session.

author: charles.mosley@gmail.com
body: I've got three nurses from Bluff City volunteering for the blood pressure station. Should we also add a general wellness table for folks who want to talk about lifestyle changes? I can coordinate with the deaconess board on supplies.

author: rebecca.price@memorialhospital-mem.org
body: Yes - let's add the wellness table. I'll ask Lisa Moore if she can staff it for two hours or at least provide handouts. The deaconess board can handle the water station and seating. This is shaping up beautifully, Charles.

author: sandra.coles@gmail.com
body: Rebecca, I can commit to the nursing station from 9 AM to noon. I'll bring two of my senior students to help - good field experience for them. Just tell me what supplies you need me to bring.

author: william.greer@gmail.com
body: Rebecca, this is beautiful. The light metaphor will land perfectly at dawn. Leave plenty of room after 'arise, shine, for your light has come' - that silence is doing work. The congregation will feel it.

author: rebecca.price@memorialhospital-mem.org
body: Thank you, Pastor Greer. I'll trust the pause. I've been practicing it in my 5 AM devotional time - it's longer than feels comfortable, which probably means it's right.

author: rebecca.price@memorialhospital-mem.org
body: Finished 'The Poisonwood Bible' this week - absolutely doing a books segment. Working title: 'What Faith Looks Like in Hard Soil.' Will draft a 4-minute segment intro for the March episode. Sandra would love this one.
